2,\n\t\tscheduler.WithProfiles(prof),\n\t\tscheduler.WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(registry))\n\n\t// Both permit plugins will return Wait for permitting\n\tperPlugin1.timeoutPermit = true\n\tperPlugin2.timeoutPermit = true\n\n\t// Create a test pod.\n\tpodName := \"test-pod\"\n\tpod, err := testutils.CreatePausePod(testCtx.ClientSet,\n\t\ttestutils.InitPausePod(&testutils.PausePodConfig{Name: podName, Namespace: testCtx.NS.Name}))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error while creating a test pod: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar waitingPod framework.WaitingPod\n\t// Wait until the test pod is actually waiting.\n\twait.PollUntilContextTimeout(testCtx.Ctx, 10*time.Millisecond, 30*time.Second, false, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n\t\twaitingPod = perPlugin1.fh.GetWaitingPod(pod.UID)\n\t\treturn waitingPod != nil, nil\n\t})\n\n\tperPlugin1.rejectAllPods()\n\t// Wait some time for the permit plugins to be cancelled\n\terr = wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(testCtx.Ctx, 10*time.Millisecond, 30*time.Second, false, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n\t\tp1 := perPlugin1.deepCopy()\n\t\tp2 := perPlugin2.deepCopy()\n\t\treturn p1.cancelled && p2.cancelled, nil\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected all permit plugins to be cancelled\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestCoSchedulingWithPermitPlugin tests invocation of permit plugins.\nfunc TestCoSchedulingWithPermitPlugin(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestContext := testutils.InitTestAPIServer(t, \"permit-plugin\", nil)\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname       string\n\t\twaitReject bool\n\t\twaitAllow  bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:       \"having wait reject true and wait allow false\",\n\t\t\twaitReject: true,\n\t\t\twaitAllow:  false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:       \"having wait reject false and wait allow true\",\n\t\t\twaitReject: false,\n\t\t\twaitAllow:  true,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, test := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\n\t\t\t// Create a plugin registry for testing. Register only a permit plugin.\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin := &PermitPlugin{name: permitPluginName}\n\t\t\tregistry, prof := initRegistryAndConfig(t, permitPlugin)\n\n\t\t\ttestCtx, teardown := schedulerutils.InitTestSchedulerForFrameworkTest(t, testContext, 2,\n\t\t\t\tscheduler.WithProfiles(prof),\n\t\t\t\tscheduler.WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(registry))\n\t\t\tdefer teardown()\n\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin.failPermit = false\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin.rejectPermit = false\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin.timeoutPermit = false\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin.waitAndRejectPermit = test.waitReject\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin.waitAndAllowPermit = test.waitAllow\n\n\t\t\t// Create two pods. First pod to enter Permit() will wait and a second one will either\n\t\t\t// reject or allow first one.\n\t\t\tpodA, err := testutils.CreatePausePod(testCtx.ClientSet,\n\t\t\t\ttestutils.InitPausePod(&testutils.PausePodConfig{Name: \"pod-a\", Namespace: testCtx.NS.Name}))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error while creating the first pod: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tpodB, err := testutils.CreatePausePod(testCtx.ClientSet,\n\t\t\t\ttestutils.InitPausePod(&testutils.PausePodConfig{Name: \"pod-b\", Namespace: testCtx.NS.Name}))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error while creating the second pod: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif test.waitReject {\n\t\t\t\tif err = testutils.WaitForPodUnschedulable(testCtx.ClientSet, podA); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Didn't expect the first pod to be scheduled. error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif err = testutils.WaitForPodUnschedulable(testCtx.ClientSet, podB); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Didn't expect the second pod to be scheduled. error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif !((permitPlugin.waitingPod == podA.Name && permitPlugin.rejectingPod == podB.Name) ||\n\t\t\t\t\t(permitPlugin.waitingPod == podB.Name && permitPlugin.rejectingPod == podA.Name)) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expect one pod to wait and another pod to reject instead %s waited and %s rejected.\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpermitPlugin.waitingPod, permitPlugin.rejectingPod)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err = testutils.WaitForPodToSchedule(testCtx.ClientSet, podA); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the first pod to be scheduled. error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif err = testutils.WaitForPodToSchedule(testCtx.ClientSet, podB); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the second pod to be scheduled. error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif !((permitPlugin.waitingPod == podA.Name && permitPlugin.allowingPod == podB.Name) ||\n\t\t\t\t\t(permitPlugin.waitingPod == podB.Name && permitPlugin.allowingPod == podA.Name)) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expect one pod to wait and another pod to allow instead %s waited and %s allowed.\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpermitPlugin.waitingPod, permitPlugin.allowingPod)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tp := permitPlugin.deepCopy()\n\t\t\tif p.numPermitCalled == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the permit plugin to be called.\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFilterPlugin tests invocation of filter plugins.\nfunc TestFilterPlugin(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestContext := testutils.InitTestAPIServer(t, \"filter-plugin\", nil)\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname string\n\t\tfail bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"fail filter plugin\",\n\t\t\tfail: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"do not fail filter plugin\",\n\t\t\tfail: false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, test := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Create a plugin registry for testing. Register only a filter plugin.\n\t\t\tfilterPlugin := &FilterPlugin{}\n\t\t\tregistry, prof := initRegistryAndConfig(t, filterPlugin)\n\n\t\t\ttestCtx, teardown := schedulerutils.InitTestSchedulerForFrameworkTest(t, testContext, 1,\n\t\t\t\tscheduler.WithProfiles(prof),\n\t\t\t\tscheduler.WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(registry))\n\t\t\tdefer teardown()\n\n\t\t\tfilterPlugin.failFilter = test.fail\n\t\t\t// Create a best effort pod.\n\t\t\tpod, err := testutils.CreatePausePod(testCtx.ClientSet,\n\t\t\t\ttestutils.InitPausePod(&testutils.PausePodConfig{Name: \"test-pod\", Namespace: testCtx.NS.Name}))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error while creating a test pod: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif test.fail {\n\t\t\t\tif err = wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(testCtx.Ctx, 10*time.Millisecond, 30*time.Second, false,\n\t\t\t\t\ttestutils.PodSchedulingError(testCtx.ClientSet, pod.Namespace, pod.Name)); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected a scheduling error, but got: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif filterPlugin.numFilterCalled < 1 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the filter plugin to be called at least 1 time, but got %v.\", filterPlugin.numFilterCalled)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err = testutils.WaitForPodToSchedule(testCtx.ClientSet, pod); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the pod to be scheduled. error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif filterPlugin.numFilterCalled != 1 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the filter plugin to be called 1 time, but got %v.\", filterPlugin.numFilterCalled)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestPreScorePlugin tests invocation of pre-score plugins.\nfunc TestPreScorePlugin(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestContext := testutils.InitTestAPIServer(t, \"pre-score-plugin\", nil)\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname string\n\t\tfail bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"fail preScore plugin\",\n\t\t\tfail: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"do not fail preScore plugin\",\n\t\t\tfail: false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, test := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Create a plugin registry for testing. err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected a scheduling error, but got: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err = testutils.WaitForPodToSchedule(testCtx.ClientSet, pod); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the pod to be scheduled. error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif preScorePlugin.numPreScoreCalled == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the pre-score plugin to be called.\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestPreEnqueuePlugin tests invocation of enqueue plugins.\nfunc TestPreEnqueuePlugin(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestContext := testutils.InitTestAPIServer(t, \"enqueue-plugin\", nil)\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname         string\n\t\tpod          *v1.Pod\n\t\tadmitEnqueue bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"pod is admitted to enqueue\",\n\t\t\tpod:          st.MakePod().Name(\"p\").Namespace(testContext.NS.Name).Container(\"pause\").Obj(),\n\t\t\tadmitEnqueue: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"pod is not admitted to enqueue\",\n\t\t\tpod:          st.MakePod().Name(\"p\").Namespace(testContext.NS.Name).SchedulingGates([]string{\"foo\"}).Container(\"pause\").Obj(),\n\t\t\tadmitEnqueue: false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Create a plugin registry for testing. Register only a filter plugin.\n\t\t\tenqueuePlugin := &PreEnqueuePlugin{}\n\t\t\t// Plumb a preFilterPlugin to verify if it's called or not.\n\t\t\tpreFilterPlugin := &PreFilterPlugin{}\n\t\t\tregistry, prof := initRegistryAndConfig(t, enqueuePlugin, preFilterPlugin)\n\n\t\t\ttestCtx, teardown := schedulerutils.InitTestSchedulerForFrameworkTest(t, testContext, 1,\n\t\t\t\tscheduler.WithProfiles(prof),\n\t\t\t\tscheduler.WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(registry))\n\t\t\tdefer teardown()\n\n\t\t\tenqueuePlugin.admit = tt.admitEnqueue\n\t\t\t// Create a best effort pod.\n\t\t\tpod, err := testutils.CreatePausePod(testCtx.ClientSet, tt.pod)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error while creating a test pod: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif tt.admitEnqueue {\n\t\t\t\tif err := testutils.WaitForPodToScheduleWithTimeout(testCtx.ClientSet, pod, 10*time.Second); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the pod to be schedulable, but got: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Also verify enqueuePlugin is called.\n\t\t\t\tif enqueuePlugin.called == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the enqueuePlugin plugin to be called at least once, but got 0\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif err := testutils.WaitForPodSchedulingGated(testCtx.ClientSet, pod, 10*time.Second); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the pod to be scheduling waiting, but got: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Also verify preFilterPlugin is not called.\n\t\t\t\tif preFilterPlugin.numPreFilterCalled != 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the preFilter plugin not to be called, but got %v\", preFilterPlugin.numPreFilterCalled)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestPreemptWithPermitPlugin tests preempt with permit plugins.\n// It verifies how waitingPods behave in different scenarios:\n// - when waitingPods get preempted\n//   - they should be removed from internal waitingPods map, but not physically deleted\n//   - it'd trigger moving unschedulable Pods, but not the waitingPods themselves\n//\n// - when waitingPods get deleted externally, it'd trigger moving unschedulable Pods\nfunc TestPreemptWithPermitPlugin(t *testing.T) {\n\ttestContext := testutils.InitTestAPIServer(t, \"preempt-with-permit-plugin\", nil)\n\n\tns := testContext.NS.Name\n\tlowPriority, highPriority := int32(100), int32(300)\n\tresReq := map[v1.ResourceName]string{\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    \"200m\",\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: \"200\",\n\t}\n\tpreemptorReq := map[v1.ResourceName]string{\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    \"400m\",\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: \"400\",\n\t}\n\n\tnodeRes := map[v1.ResourceName]string{\n\t\tv1.ResourcePods:   \"32\",\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    \"500m\",\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: \"500\",\n\t}\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname                   string\n\t\tdeleteWaitingPod       bool\n\t\tmaxNumWaitingPodCalled int\n\t\trunningPod             *v1.Pod\n\t\twaitingPod             *v1.Pod\n\t\tpreemptor              *v1.Pod\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:                   \"waiting pod is not physically deleted upon preemption\",\n\t\t\tmaxNumWaitingPodCalled: 2,\n\t\t\trunningPod:             st.MakePod().Name(\"running-pod\").Namespace(ns).Priority(lowPriority).Req(resReq).ZeroTerminationGracePeriod().Obj(),\n\t\t\twaitingPod:             st.MakePod().Name(\"waiting-pod\").Namespace(ns).Priority(lowPriority).Req(resReq).ZeroTerminationGracePeriod().Obj(),\n\t\t\tpreemptor:              st.MakePod().Name(\"preemptor-pod\").Namespace(ns).Priority(highPriority).Req(preemptorReq).ZeroTerminationGracePeriod().Obj(),\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\t// The waiting Pod has once gone through the scheduling cycle,\n\t\t\t// and we don't know if it's schedulable or not after it's preempted.\n\t\t\t// So, we should retry the scheduling of it so that it won't stuck in the unschedulable Pod pool.\n\t\t\tname:                   \"rejecting a waiting pod to trigger retrying unschedulable pods immediately, and the waiting pod itself will be retried\",\n\t\t\tmaxNumWaitingPodCalled: 2,\n\t\t\twaitingPod:             st.MakePod().Name(\"waiting-pod\").Namespace(ns).Priority(lowPriority).Req(resReq).ZeroTerminationGracePeriod().Obj(),\n\t\t\tpreemptor:              st.MakePod().Name(\"preemptor-pod\").Namespace(ns).Priority(highPriority).Req(preemptorReq).ZeroTerminationGracePeriod().Obj(),\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:                   \"deleting a waiting pod to trigger retrying unschedulable pods immediately\",\n\t\t\tdeleteWaitingPod:       true,\n\t\t\tmaxNumWaitingPodCalled: 1,\n\t\t\twaitingPod:             st.MakePod().Name(\"waiting-pod\").Namespace(ns).Priority(lowPriority).Req(resReq).ZeroTerminationGracePeriod().Obj(),\n\t\t\tpreemptor:              st.MakePod().Name(\"preemptor-pod\").Namespace(ns).Priority(lowPriority).Req(preemptorReq).ZeroTerminationGracePeriod().Obj(),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t// Create a plugin registry for testing. Register a permit and a filter plugin.\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin := &PermitPlugin{}\n\t\t\t// Inject a fake filter plugin to use its internal `numFilterCalled` to verify\n\t\t\t// how many times a Pod gets tried scheduling.\n\t\t\tfilterPlugin := &FilterPlugin{numCalledPerPod: make(map[string]int)}\n\t\t\tregistry := frameworkruntime.Registry{\n\t\t\t\tpermitPluginName: newPlugin(permitPlugin),\n\t\t\t\tfilterPluginName: newPlugin(filterPlugin),\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Setup initial permit and filter plugins in the profile.\n\t\t\tcfg := configtesting.V1ToInternalWithDefaults(t, configv1.KubeSchedulerConfiguration{\n\t\t\t\tProfiles: []configv1.KubeSchedulerProfile{\n\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tSchedulerName: pointer.String(v1.DefaultSchedulerName),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tPlugins: &configv1.Plugins{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tPermit: configv1.PluginSet{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tEnabled: []configv1.Plugin{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Name: permitPluginName},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFilter: configv1.PluginSet{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Ensure the fake filter plugin is always called; otherwise noderesources\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// would fail first and exit the Filter phase.\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tEnabled: []configv1.Plugin{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Name: filterPluginName},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Name: noderesources.Name},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tDisabled: []configv1.Plugin{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{Name: noderesources.Name},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t})\n\n\t\t\ttestCtx, teardown := schedulerutils.InitTestSchedulerForFrameworkTest(t, testContext, 0,\n\t\t\t\tscheduler.WithProfiles(cfg.Profiles...),\n\t\t\t\tscheduler.WithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(registry),\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\tdefer teardown()\n\n\t\t\t_, err := testutils.CreateAndWaitForNodesInCache(testCtx, \"test-node\", st.MakeNode().Capacity(nodeRes), 1)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin.waitAndAllowPermit = true\n\t\t\tpermitPlugin.waitingPod = \"waiting-pod\"\n\n\t\t\tif r := tt.runningPod; r != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif _, err := testutils.CreatePausePod(testCtx.ClientSet, r); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error while creating the running pod: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Wait until the pod to be scheduled.\n\t\t\t\tif err = testutils.WaitForPodToSchedule(testCtx.ClientSet, r); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"The running pod is expected to be scheduled: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif w := tt.waitingPod; w != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif _, err := testutils.CreatePausePod(testCtx.ClientSet, w); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error while creating the waiting pod: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Wait until the waiting-pod is actually waiting.\n\t\t\t\tif err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(testCtx.Ctx, 10*time.Millisecond, 30*time.Second, false, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n\t\t\t\t\tw := false\n\t\t\t\t\tpermitPlugin.fh.IterateOverWaitingPods(func(wp framework.WaitingPod) { w = true })\n\t\t\t\t\treturn w, nil\n\t\t\t\t}); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"The waiting pod is expected to be waiting: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif p := tt.preemptor; p != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif _, err := testutils.CreatePausePod(testCtx.ClientSet, p); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error while creating the preemptor pod: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// Delete the waiting pod if specified.\n\t\t\t\tif w := tt.waitingPod; w != nil && tt.deleteWaitingPod {\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutils.DeletePod(testCtx.ClientSet, w.Name, w.Namespace); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error while deleting the waiting pod: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif err = testutils.WaitForPodToSchedule(testCtx.ClientSet, p); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Expected the preemptor pod to be scheduled. error: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif w := tt.waitingPod; w != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif err := wait.PollUntilContextTimeout(testCtx.Ctx, 200*time.Millisecond, wait.ForeverTestTimeout, false, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {\n\t\t\t\t\tw := false\n\t\t\t\t\tpermitPlugin.fh.IterateOverWaitingPods(func(wp framework.WaitingPod) { w = true })\n\t\t\t\t\treturn !w, nil\n\t\t\t\t}); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Expected the waiting pod to get preempted.\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tp := filterPlugin.deepCopy()\n\t\t\t\twaitingPodCalled := p.numCalledPerPod[fmt.Sprintf(\"%v/%v\", w.Namespace, w.Name)]\n\t\t\t\tif waitingPodCalled > tt.maxNumWaitingPodCalled {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Expected the waiting pod to be called %v times at most, but got %v\", tt.maxNumWaitingPodCalled, waitingPodCalled)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif !tt.deleteWaitingPod {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Expect the waitingPod to be still present.\n\t\t\t\t\tif _, err := testutils.GetPod(testCtx.ClientSet, w.Name, w.Namespace); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Error(\"Get waiting pod in waiting pod failed.\")\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif permitPlugin.numPermitCalled == 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected the permit plugin to be called.\")\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif r := tt.runningPod; node which has anti affinity match will get high score\",\n\t\t\tpod:  &v1.Pod{Spec: v1.PodSpec{NodeName: \"\"}, ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Labels: podLabelSecurityS2}},\n\t\t\tpods: []*v1.Pod{\n\t\t\t\t{Spec: v1.PodSpec{NodeName: \"node1\", Affinity: awayFromS2InAz}, ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Labels: podLabelSecurityS1}},\n\t\t\t\t{Spec: v1.PodSpec{NodeName: \"node2\", labelAzAz1}},\n\t\t\t\t{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"node2\", Labels: labelAzAz2}},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\texpectedList: []framework.NodeScore{{Name: \"node1\", Score: 0}, {Name: \"node2\", Score: framework.MaxNodeScore}},\n\t\t},\n\t\t// Test both  affinity and anti-affinity\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"Affinity and Anti Affinity: considered only preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution in podLabelSecurityS1}},\n\t\t\tpods: []*v1.Pod{\n\t\t\t\t{Spec: v1.PodSpec{NodeName: \"node1\"}, ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Labels: podLabelSecurityS1}},\n\t\t\t\t{Spec: v1.PodSpec{NodeName: \"node2\"}, ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Labels: podLabelSecurityS1}},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tnodes: []*v1.Node{\n\t\t\t\t{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"node1\", Labels: {Name: \"node2\", Score: 0}},\n\t\t},\n\t\t// Combined cases considering both affinity and anti-affinity, the pod to schedule and existing pods have the same labels (they are in the same RC/service),\n\t\t// the pod prefer to run together with its brother pods in the same region, but wants to stay away from them at node level,\n\t\t// so that all the pods of a RC/service can stay in a _, test := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(test.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t_, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\t\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t\t\tstate := framework.NewCycleState()\n\t\t\tp := plugintesting.SetupPluginWithInformers(ctx, t, New, &config.InterPodAffinityArgs{HardPodAffinityWeight: 1, IgnorePreferredTermsOfExistingPods: test.ignorePreferredTermsOfExistingPods}, cache.NewSnapshot(test.pods, test.nodes), namespaces)\n\t\t\tstatus := p.(framework.PreScorePlugin).PreScore(ctx, state, test.pod, tf.BuildNodeInfos(test.nodes))\n\n\t\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\t\tif status.Code() != test.wantStatus.Code() {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"InterPodAffinity#PreScore() returned unexpected status.Code got: %v, want: %v\", status.Code(), test.wantStatus.Code())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(status.Message(), test.wantStatus.Message()) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"InterPodAffinity#PreScore() {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"unexpected error from Score: %v\", status)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tgotList = append(gotList, framework.NodeScore{Name: nodeName, Score: score})\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tstatus = p.(framework.ScorePlugin).ScoreExtensions().NormalizeScore(ctx, state, test.pod, gotList)\n\t\t\tif !status.IsSuccess() {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"unexpected error from NormalizeScore: %v\", status)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif diff := cmp.Diff(test.expectedList, gotList); diff != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"node score list doesn't match (-want,+got): \\n %s\", diff)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestPreferredAffinityWithHardPodAffinitySymmetricWeight(t *testing.T) {\n\tpodLabelServiceS1 := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"service\": \"S1\",\n\t}\n\tlabelRgChina \"subteam2.team2\"},\n\t\t\t\t\tNamespaceSelector: &metav1.LabelSelector{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tMatchExpressions: []metav1.LabelSelectorRequirement{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKey:      \"team\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tOperator: metav1.LabelSelectorOpIn,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tValues:   []string{\"team1\"},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\tTopologyKey: \"region\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tpod                   *v1.Pod\n\t\tpods                  []*v1.Pod\n\t\tnodes \"with default weight\",\n\t\t\tpod:  &v1.Pod{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Labels: podLabelServiceS1}},\n\t\t\tpods: []*v1.Pod{\n\t\t\t\t{Spec: v1.PodSpec{NodeName: \"node1\", Affinity: labelAzAz1}},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\thardPodAffinityWeight: v1.DefaultHardPodAffinitySymmetricWeight,\n\t\t\texpectedList:          []framework.NodeScore{{Name: \"node1\", Score: framework.MaxNodeScore}, TestSchedulerGuaranteeNonNilNodeInSchedulingCycle(t *testing.T) {\n\trandom := rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano()))\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tinitialNodeNumber        = 1000\n\t\tinitialPodNumber         = 500\n\t\twaitSchedulingPodNumber  = 200\n\t\tdeleteNodeNumberPerRound = 20\n\t\tcreatePodNumberPerRound  = 50\n\n\t\tfakeSchedulerName = \"fake-scheduler\"\n\t\tfakeNamespace     = \"fake-namespace\"\n\n\t\tinitialNodes []runtime.Object\n\t\tinitialPods  []runtime.Object\n\t)\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < initialNodeNumber; i++ {\n\t\tnodeName := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%d\", i)\n\t\tinitialNodes = append(initialNodes, st.MakeNode().Name(nodeName).UID(nodeName).Obj())\n\t}\n\t// Randomly scatter initial pods onto nodes.\n\tfor i := 0; i < initialPodNumber; i++ {\n\t\tpodName := fmt.Sprintf(\"scheduled-pod%d\", i)\n\t\tassignedNodeName := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%d\", random.Intn(initialNodeNumber))\n\t\tinitialPods = append(initialPods, st.MakePod().Name(podName).UID(podName).Node(assignedNodeName).Obj())\n\t}\n\n\tobjs := []runtime.Object{&v1.Namespace{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: fakeNamespace}}}\n\tobjs = append(objs, initialNodes...)\n\tobjs = append(objs, initialPods...)\n\tclient := clientsetfake.NewSimpleClientset(objs...)\n\tbroadcaster := events.NewBroadcaster(&events.EventSinkImpl{Interface: client.EventsV1()})\n\n\tinformerFactory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(client, 0)\n\tsched, err := New(\n\t\tctx,\n\t\tclient,\n\t\tinformerFactory,\n\t\tnil,\n\t\tprofile.NewRecorderFactory(broadcaster),\n\t\tWithProfiles(\n\t\t\tschedulerapi.KubeSchedulerProfile{SchedulerName: fakeSchedulerName,\n\t\t\t\tPlugins: &schedulerapi.Plugins{\n\t\t\t\t\tFilter:    schedulerapi.PluginSet{Enabled: []schedulerapi.Plugin{{Name: \"FakeNodeSelectorDependOnPodAnnotation\"}}},\n\t\t\t\t\tQueueSort: schedulerapi.PluginSet{Enabled: []schedulerapi.Plugin{{Name: \"PrioritySort\"}}},\n\t\t\t\t\tBind:      schedulerapi.PluginSet{Enabled: []schedulerapi.Plugin{{Name: \"DefaultBinder\"}}},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t),\n\t\tWithFrameworkOutOfTreeRegistry(frameworkruntime.Registry{\n\t\t\t\"FakeNodeSelectorDependOnPodAnnotation\": newFakeNodeSelectorDependOnPodAnnotation,\n\t\t}),\n\t)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run scheduler.\n\tinformerFactory.Start(ctx.Done())\n\tinformerFactory.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done())\n\tgo sched.Run(ctx)\n\n\tvar deleteNodeIndex int\n\tdeleteNodesOneRound := func() {\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < deleteNodeNumberPerRound; i++ {\n\t\t\tif deleteNodeIndex >= initialNodeNumber {\n\t\t\t\t// all initial nodes are already deleted\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdeleteNodeName := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%d\", deleteNodeIndex)\n\t\t\tif err := client.CoreV1().Nodes().Delete(ctx, deleteNodeName, metav1.DeleteOptions{}); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdeleteNodeIndex++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tvar createPodIndex int\n\tcreatePodsOneRound := func() {\n\t\tif createPodIndex > waitSchedulingPodNumber {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < createPodNumberPerRound; i++ {\n\t\t\tpodName := fmt.Sprintf(\"pod%d\", createPodIndex)\n\t\t\t// Note: the node(specifiedNodeName) may already be deleted, which leads pod scheduled failed.\n\t\t\tspecifiedNodeName := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%d\", random.Intn(initialNodeNumber))\n\n\t\t\twaitSchedulingPod := st.MakePod().Namespace(fakeNamespace).Name(podName).UID(podName).Annotation(fakeSpecifiedNodeNameAnnotation, specifiedNodeName).SchedulerName(fakeSchedulerName).Obj()\n\t\t\tif _, err := client.CoreV1().Pods(fakeNamespace).Create(ctx, waitSchedulingPod, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcreatePodIndex++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Following we start 2 goroutines asynchronously to detect potential racing issues:\n\t// 1) One is responsible for deleting several nodes in each round;\n\t// 2) Another is creating several pods in each round to trigger scheduling;\n\t// Those two goroutines will stop until ctx.Done() is called, which means all waiting pods are scheduled at least once.\n\tgo wait.Until(deleteNodesOneRound, 10*time.Millisecond, ctx.Done())\n\tgo wait.Until(createPodsOneRound, 9*time.Millisecond, ctx.Done())\n\n\t// Capture the events to wait all pods to be scheduled at least once.\n\tallWaitSchedulingPods := sets.New[string]()\n\tfor i := 0; i < waitSchedulingPodNumber; i++ {\n\t\tallWaitSchedulingPods.Insert(fmt.Sprintf(\"pod%d\", i))\n\t}\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\twg.Add(waitSchedulingPodNumber)\n\tstopFn, err := broadcaster.StartEventWatcher(func(obj runtime.Object) {\n\t\te, ok := obj.(*eventsv1.Event)\n\t\tif !ok || (e.Reason != \"Scheduled\" && e.Reason != \"FailedScheduling\") {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif allWaitSchedulingPods.Has(e.Regarding.Name) {\n\t\t\twg.Done()\n\t\t\tallWaitSchedulingPods.Delete(e.Regarding.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\tdefer stopFn()\n\n\twg.Wait()\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerScheduleOne(t []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, queuedPodStore, scache, pod, &node, fns...)\n\n\twaitPodExpireChan := make(chan struct{})\n\ttimeout := make(chan struct{})\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tfor {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-timeout:\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tpods, err := scache.PodCount()\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"cache.List failed: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif pods == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tclose(waitPodExpireChan)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\t// waiting for the assumed pod to expire\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\tcase <-waitPodExpireChan:\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tclose(timeout)\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout timeout in waiting pod expire after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n\n\t// We use conflicted pod ports to incur fit predicate failure if first pod not removed.\n\tsecondPod := podWithPort(\"bar\", \"\", 8080)\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase b := <-bindingChan:\n\t\texpectBinding := &v1.Binding{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"bar\", UID: types.UID(\"bar\")},\n\t\t\tTarget:     v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"Node\", Name: node.Name},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectBinding, b) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"binding want=%v, get=%v\", expectBinding, b)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in binding after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerNoPhantomPodAfterDelete(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\tqueuedPodStore := clientcache.NewFIFO(clientcache.MetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\tscache := internalcache.New(ctx, 10*time.Minute)\n\tfirstPod := podWithPort(\"pod.Name\", \"\", 8080)\n\tnode := v1.Node{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"node1\", UID: types.UID(\"node1\")}}\n\tscache.AddNode(logger, &node)\n\tfns := []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterPluginAsExtensions(nodeports.Name, nodeports.New, \"Filter\", \"PreFilter\"),\n\t}\n\tscheduler, bindingChan, errChan := setupTestSchedulerWithOnePodOnNode(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, firstPod, &node, fns...)\n\n\t// We use conflicted pod ports to incur fit predicate failure.\n\tsecondPod := podWithPort(\"bar\", \"\", 8080)\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\t// queuedPodStore: [bar:8080]\n\t// cache: [(assumed)foo:8080]\n\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\texpectErr := &framework.FitError{\n\t\t\tPod:         secondPod,\n\t\t\tNumAllNodes: 1,\n\t\t\tDiagnosis: framework.Diagnosis{\n\t\t\t\tNodeToStatusMap: framework.NodeToStatusMap{\n\t\t\t\t\tnode.Name: framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, nodeports.ErrReason).WithPlugin(nodeports.Name),\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tUnschedulablePlugins: sets.New(nodeports.Name),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectErr, err) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err want=%v, get=%v\", expectErr, err)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in fitting after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n\n\t// We mimic the workflow of cache behavior when a pod is removed by user.\n\t// Note: if the schedulernodeinfo timeout would be super short, the first pod would expire\n\t// and would be removed itself (without any explicit actions on schedulernodeinfo). Even in that case,\n\t// explicitly AddPod will as well correct the behavior.\n\tfirstPod.Spec.NodeName = node.Name\n\tif err := scache.AddPod(logger, firstPod); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif err := scache.RemovePod(logger, firstPod); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase b := <-bindingChan:\n\t\texpectBinding := &v1.Binding{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"bar\", UID: types.UID(\"bar\")},\n\t\t\tTarget:     v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"Node\", Name: node.Name},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectBinding, b) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"binding want=%v, get=%v\", expectBinding, b)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in binding after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerFailedSchedulingReasons(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\tqueuedPodStore := clientcache.NewFIFO(clientcache.MetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\tscache := internalcache.New(ctx, 10*time.Minute)\n\n\t// Design the baseline for the pods, and we will make nodes that don't fit it later.\n\tvar cpu = int64(4)\n\tvar mem = int64(500)\n\tpodWithTooBigResourceRequests := podWithResources(\"bar\", \"\", v1.ResourceList{\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t}, v1.ResourceList{\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t})\n\n\t// create several nodes which cannot schedule the above pod\n\tvar nodes []*v1.Node\n\tvar objects []runtime.Object\n\tfor i := 0; i < 100; i++ {\n\t\tuid := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%v\", i)\n\t\tnode := v1.Node{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: uid, UID: types.UID(uid)},\n\t\t\tStatus: v1.NodeStatus{\n\t\t\t\tCapacity: v1.ResourceList{\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu/2, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem/5, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourcePods:   *(resource.NewQuantity(10, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tAllocatable: v1.ResourceList{\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu/2, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem/5, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourcePods:   *(resource.NewQuantity(10, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t}},\n\t\t}\n\t\tscache.AddNode(logger, &node)\n\t\tnodes = append(nodes, &node)\n\t\tobjects = append(objects, &node)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create expected failure reasons for all the nodes. Hopefully they will get rolled up into a non-spammy summary.\n\tfailedNodeStatues := framework.NodeToStatusMap{}\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tfailedNodeStatues[node.Name] = v1.ResourceMemory),\n\t\t).WithPlugin(noderesources.Name)\n\t}\n\tfns := []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterPluginAsExtensions(noderesources.Name, frameworkruntime.FactoryAdapter(feature.Features{}, noderesources.NewFit), \"Filter\", \"PreFilter\"),\n\t}\n\n\tinformerFactory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(clientsetfake.NewSimpleClientset(objects...), 0)\n\tscheduler, _, errChan := setupTestScheduler(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, informerFactory, nil, fns...)\n\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(podWithTooBigResourceRequests)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\texpectErr := &framework.FitError{\n\t\t\tPod:         podWithTooBigResourceRequests,\n\t\t\tNumAllNodes: len(nodes),\n\t\t\tDiagnosis: framework.Diagnosis{\n\t\t\t\tNodeToStatusMap:      failedNodeStatues,\n\t\t\t\tUnschedulablePlugins: sets.New(noderesources.Name),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(fmt.Sprint(expectErr)) > 150 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"message is too spammy ! %v \", len(fmt.Sprint(expectErr)))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectErr, err) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"\\n err \\nWANT=%+v,\\nGOT=%+v\", expectErr, err)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerWithVolumeBinding(t *testing.T) {\n\tfindErr := fmt.Errorf(\"find %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tclose(eventChan)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ts.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\t\t\t// Wait for pod to succeed or fail wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tstopFunc()\n\t\t\t// Wait for scheduling to return an error or succeed binding.\n\t\t\tvar (\n\t\t\t\tgotErr  error\n\t\t\t\tgotBind error after %v\", chanTimeout)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif item.expectError != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif gotErr == nil || item.expectError.Error() != gotErr.Error() {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err !cmp.Equal(item.expectPodBind, gotBind) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err \\nWANT=%+v,\\nGOT=%+v\", item.expectPodBind, gotBind)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif item.expectAssumeCalled != fakeVolumeBinder.AssumeCalled {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expectedAssumeCall %v\", item.expectAssumeCalled)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif item.expectBindCalled != fakeVolumeBinder.BindCalled {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expectedBindCall %v\", item.expectBindCalled)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerBinding(t *testing.T) {\n\ttable := []struct {\n\t\tpodName      string\n\t\textenders    []framework.Extender\n\t\twantBinderID err)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif diff := cmp.Diff(tt.expectedNodes, nodes); diff != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"filtered nodes (-want,+got):\\n%s\", diff)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif diff := {\n\t\t\t\twantEvaluatedNodes = int(*test.wantEvaluatedNodes)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif test.wErr == nil && wantEvaluatedNodes != result.EvaluatedNodes {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected EvaluatedNodes: %d, got: %d\", wantEvaluatedNodes, result.EvaluatedNodes)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFindFitAllError(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer           *resource.NewQuantity(100, resource.DecimalSI),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tAllocatable: v1.ResourceList{\n\n\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *resource.NewMilliQuantity(milliCPU, resource.DecimalSI),\n\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *resource.NewQuantity(memory, resource.BinarySI),\n\t\t\t\t\"pods\":            *resource.NewQuantity(100, resource.DecimalSI),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tImages: images,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// queuedPodStore: pods queued before processing.\n// cache: scheduler cache that might contain assumed pods.\nfunc setupTestSchedulerWithOnePodOnNode(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T, queuedPodStore *clientcache.FIFO, scache internalcache.Cache,\n\tpod *v1.Pod, node *v1.Node, fns ...tf.RegisterPluginFunc) (*Scheduler, chan *v1.Binding, chan error) {\n\tscheduler, bindingChan, errChan := setupTestScheduler(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, nil, nil, fns...)\n\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(pod)\n\t// queuedPodStore: [foo:8080]\n\t// cache: []\n\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\t// queuedPodStore: []\n\t// cache: [(assumed)foo:8080]\n\n\tselect {\n\tcase b := <-bindingChan:\n\t\texpectBinding := &v1.Binding{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: pod.Name, UID: types.UID(pod.Name)},\n\t\t\tTarget:     v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"Node\", Name: node.Name},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectBinding, b) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"binding want=%v, get=%v\", expectBinding, b)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n\treturn scheduler, bindingChan, errChan\n}\n\n// queuedPodStore: pods queued before processing.\n// scache: scheduler cache that might contain assumed pods.\nfunc setupTestScheduler(ctx context.Context, t *testing.T, queuedPodStore *clientcache.FIFO, cache internalcache.Cache, informerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory, broadcaster events.EventBroadcaster, framework.Handle) (framework.Plugin, error) {\n\treturn &noPodsFilterPlugin{}, nil\n}\n\ntype reverseNumericMapPlugin struct{}\n\nfunc (pl *reverseNumericMapPlugin) Name() string {\n\treturn \"ReverseNumericMap\"\n}\n\nfunc (pl *reverseNumericMapPlugin) Score(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, _ *v1.Pod, nodeName string) (int64, *framework.Status) {\n\tscore, err := nil\n}\n\nfunc (pl *reverseNumericMapPlugin) ScoreNormalizer() framework.ScoreNormalizer {\n\treturn pl\n}\n\nfunc (pl *reverseNumericMapPlugin) NormalizeScore(_ context.Context, _ *framework.CycleState, _ *v1.Pod, nodeScores framework.NodeScoreList) *framework.Status {\n\tvar maxScore float64\n\tminScore := math.MaxFloat64\n\n\tfor _, hostPriority := range nodeScores {\n\t\tmaxScore = math.Max(maxScore, float64(hostPriority.Score))\n\t\tminScore = math.Min(minScore, float64(hostPriority.Score))\n\t}\n\tfor i, hostPriority := range nodeScores {\n\t\tnodeScores[i] = framework.NodeScore{\n\t\t\tName:  hostPriority.Name,\n\t\t\tScore: int64(maxScore + minScore - float64(hostPriority.Score)),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc newReverseNumericMapPlugin() TestSchedulerGuaranteeNonNilNodeInSchedulingCycle(t *testing.T) {\n\trandom := rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano()))\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tinitialNodeNumber        = 1000\n\t\tinitialPodNumber         = 500\n\t\twaitSchedulingPodNumber  = 200\n\t\tdeleteNodeNumberPerRound = 20\n\t\tcreatePodNumberPerRound  = onto nodes.\n\tfor i := 0; i < initialPodNumber; i++ {\n\t\tpodName := fmt.Sprintf(\"scheduled-pod%d\", i)\n\t\tassignedNodeName := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%d\", random.Intn(initialNodeNumber))\n\t\tinitialPods = append(initialPods, st.MakePod().Name(podName).UID(podName).Node(assignedNodeName).Obj())\n\t}\n\n\tobjs := \"FakeNodeSelectorDependOnPodAnnotation\"}}},\n\t\t\t\t\tQueueSort: schedulerapi.PluginSet{Enabled: []schedulerapi.Plugin{{Name: \"PrioritySort\"}}},\n\t\t\t\t\tBind: {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < createPodNumberPerRound; i++ {\n\t\t\tpodName := fmt.Sprintf(\"pod%d\", createPodIndex)\n\t\t\t// Note: the node(specifiedNodeName) may already be deleted, st.MakePod().Namespace(fakeNamespace).Name(podName).UID(podName).Annotation(fakeSpecifiedNodeNameAnnotation, specifiedNodeName).SchedulerName(fakeSchedulerName).Obj()\n\t\t\tif _, err := goroutines asynchronously to detect potential racing issues:\n\t// 1) One is responsible for deleting several nodes in each round;\n\t// 2) Another is creating several pods in each round to trigger scheduling;\n\t// Those two goroutines will stop until ctx.Done() is called, which means all waiting pods are scheduled at least once.\n\tgo wait.Until(deleteNodesOneRound, 10*time.Millisecond, ctx.Done())\n\tgo wait.Until(createPodsOneRound, 9*time.Millisecond, ctx.Done())\n\n\t// Capture the events to wait all pods to be scheduled at least once.\n\tallWaitSchedulingPods := sync.WaitGroup\n\twg.Add(waitSchedulingPodNumber)\n\tstopFn, err := broadcaster.StartEventWatcher(func(obj runtime.Object) {\n\t\te, ok := obj.(*eventsv1.Event)\n\t\tif !ok || (e.Reason != \"Scheduled\" && e.Reason != \"FailedScheduling\") {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif allWaitSchedulingPods.Has(e.Regarding.Name) {\n\t\t\twg.Done()\n\t\t\tallWaitSchedulingPods.Delete(e.Regarding.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t}\n\tdefer stopFn()\n\n\twg.Wait()\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerScheduleOne(t *v1.Pod\n\t\t\tvar gotAssumedPod *v1.Pod\n\t\t\tvar gotBinding *v1.Binding\n\t\t\tcache := &fakecache.Cache{\n\t\t\t\tForgetFunc: func(pod *v1.Pod) {\n\t\t\t\t\tgotForgetPod = pod\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tAssumeFunc: func(pod *v1.Pod) {\n\t\t\t\t\tgotAssumedPod = pod\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tIsAssumedPodFunc: func(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\n\t\t\t\t\tif pod == nil || gotAssumedPod == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn pod.UID == gotAssumedPod.UID\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tclient := clientsetfake.NewSimpleClientset(item.sendPod)\n\t\t\tclient.PrependReactor(\"create\", \"pods\", func(action clienttesting.Action) (bool, runtime.Object, error) {\n\t\t\t\tif action.GetSubresource() != \"binding\" {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn false, nil, nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tgotBinding = action.(clienttesting.CreateAction).GetObject().(*v1.Binding)\n\t\t\t\treturn true, gotBinding, item.injectBindError\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tregisterPluginFuncs := append(item.registerPluginFuncs,\n\t\t\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\t\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\t\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t\t\tfwk, err := testSchedulerName)))\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tsched := &Scheduler{\n\t\t\t\tCache:  cache,\n\t\t\t\tclient: client,\n\t\t\t\tNextPod: func(logger klog.Logger) (*framework.QueuedPodInfo, error) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn &framework.QueuedPodInfo{PodInfo: mustNewPodInfo(t, item.sendPod)}, nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tSchedulingQueue: internalqueue.NewTestQueue(ctx, nil),\n\t\t\t\tProfiles:        profile.Map{testSchedulerName: fwk},\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tsched.SchedulePod = func(ctx context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, state *framework.CycleState, pod *v1.Pod) (ScheduleResult, error) {\n\t\t\t\treturn item.mockResult.result, item.mockResult.err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsched.FailureHandler = func(_ context.Context, fwk framework.Framework, p *framework.QueuedPodInfo, status *framework.Status, _ *framework.NominatingInfo, _ time.Time) {\n\t\t\t\tgotPod = p.Pod\n\t\t\t\tgotError = status.AsError()\n\n\t\t\t\tmsg := truncateMessage(gotError.Error())\n\t\t\t\tfwk.EventRecorder().Eventf(p.Pod, nil, v1.EventTypeWarning, \"FailedScheduling\", \"Scheduling\", msg)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcalled := make(chan struct{})\n\t\t\tstopFunc, err := eventBroadcaster.StartEventWatcher(func(obj runtime.Object) {\n\t\t\t\te, _ := obj.(*eventsv1.Event)\n\t\t\t\tif e.Reason != item.eventReason {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"got event %v, want %v\", e.Reason, item.eventReason)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tclose(called)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsched.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\t\t\t<-called\n\t\t\tif e, a := item.expectAssumedPod, gotAssumedPod; !reflect.DeepEqual(e, a) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"assumed pod: wanted %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif e, a := item.expectErrorPod, gotPod; !reflect.DeepEqual(e, a) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"error pod: wanted %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif e, a := item.expectForgetPod, gotForgetPod; !reflect.DeepEqual(e, a) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"forget pod: wanted %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif e, a := item.expectError, gotError; !reflect.DeepEqual(e, a) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"error: wanted %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif diff := cmp.Diff(item.expectBind, gotBinding); diff != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"got binding diff (-want, +got): %s\", diff)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tstopFunc()\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerNoPhantomPodAfterExpire(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\tqueuedPodStore := clientcache.NewFIFO(clientcache.MetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\tscache := internalcache.New(ctx, 100*time.Millisecond)\n\tpod := podWithPort(\"pod.Name\", \"\", 8080)\n\tnode := v1.Node{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"node1\", UID: types.UID(\"node1\")}}\n\tscache.AddNode(logger, &node)\n\n\tfns := []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterPluginAsExtensions(nodeports.Name, nodeports.New, \"Filter\", \"PreFilter\"),\n\t}\n\tscheduler, bindingChan, errChan := setupTestSchedulerWithOnePodOnNode(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, pod, &node, fns...)\n\n\twaitPodExpireChan := make(chan struct{})\n\ttimeout := make(chan struct{})\n\tgo func() {\n\t\tfor {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-timeout:\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tpods, err := scache.PodCount()\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"cache.List failed: %v\", err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif pods == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tclose(waitPodExpireChan)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttime.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\t// waiting for the assumed pod to expire\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\tcase <-waitPodExpireChan:\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tclose(timeout)\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout timeout in waiting pod expire after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n\n\t// We use conflicted pod ports to incur fit predicate failure if first pod not removed.\n\tsecondPod := podWithPort(\"bar\", \"\", 8080)\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase b := <-bindingChan:\n\t\texpectBinding := &v1.Binding{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"bar\", UID: types.UID(\"bar\")},\n\t\t\tTarget:     v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"Node\", Name: node.Name},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectBinding, b) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"binding want=%v, get=%v\", expectBinding, b)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in binding after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerNoPhantomPodAfterDelete(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\tqueuedPodStore := clientcache.NewFIFO(clientcache.MetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\tscache := internalcache.New(ctx, 10*time.Minute)\n\tfirstPod := podWithPort(\"pod.Name\", \"\", 8080)\n\tnode := v1.Node{ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"node1\", UID: types.UID(\"node1\")}}\n\tscache.AddNode(logger, &node)\n\tfns := []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterPluginAsExtensions(nodeports.Name, nodeports.New, \"Filter\", \"PreFilter\"),\n\t}\n\tscheduler, bindingChan, errChan := setupTestSchedulerWithOnePodOnNode(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, firstPod, &node, fns...)\n\n\t// We use conflicted pod ports to incur fit predicate failure.\n\tsecondPod := podWithPort(\"bar\", \"\", 8080)\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\t// queuedPodStore: [bar:8080]\n\t// cache: [(assumed)foo:8080]\n\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\texpectErr := &framework.FitError{\n\t\t\tPod:         secondPod,\n\t\t\tNumAllNodes: 1,\n\t\t\tDiagnosis: framework.Diagnosis{\n\t\t\t\tNodeToStatusMap: framework.NodeToStatusMap{\n\t\t\t\t\tnode.Name: framework.NewStatus(framework.Unschedulable, nodeports.ErrReason).WithPlugin(nodeports.Name),\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tUnschedulablePlugins: sets.New(nodeports.Name),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectErr, err) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err want=%v, get=%v\", expectErr, err)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in fitting after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n\n\t// We mimic the workflow of cache behavior when a pod is removed by user.\n\t// Note: if the schedulernodeinfo timeout would be super short, the first pod would expire\n\t// and would be removed itself (without any explicit actions on schedulernodeinfo). Even in that case,\n\t// explicitly AddPod will as well correct the behavior.\n\tfirstPod.Spec.NodeName = node.Name\n\tif err := scache.AddPod(logger, firstPod); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\tif err := scache.RemovePod(logger, firstPod); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(secondPod)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase b := <-bindingChan:\n\t\texpectBinding := &v1.Binding{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"bar\", UID: types.UID(\"bar\")},\n\t\t\tTarget:     v1.ObjectReference{Kind: \"Node\", Name: node.Name},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectBinding, b) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"binding want=%v, get=%v\", expectBinding, b)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout in binding after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerFailedSchedulingReasons(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger, ctx := ktesting.NewTestContext(t)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\tqueuedPodStore := clientcache.NewFIFO(clientcache.MetaNamespaceKeyFunc)\n\tscache := internalcache.New(ctx, 10*time.Minute)\n\n\t// Design the baseline for the pods, and we will make nodes that don't fit it later.\n\tvar cpu = int64(4)\n\tvar mem = int64(500)\n\tpodWithTooBigResourceRequests := podWithResources(\"bar\", \"\", v1.ResourceList{\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t}, v1.ResourceList{\n\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t})\n\n\t// create several nodes which cannot schedule the above pod\n\tvar nodes []*v1.Node\n\tvar objects []runtime.Object\n\tfor i := 0; i < 100; i++ {\n\t\tuid := fmt.Sprintf(\"node%v\", i)\n\t\tnode := v1.Node{\n\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: uid, UID: types.UID(uid)},\n\t\t\tStatus: v1.NodeStatus{\n\t\t\t\tCapacity: v1.ResourceList{\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu/2, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem/5, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourcePods:   *(resource.NewQuantity(10, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tAllocatable: v1.ResourceList{\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceCPU:    *(resource.NewQuantity(cpu/2, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourceMemory: *(resource.NewQuantity(mem/5, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t\tv1.ResourcePods:   *(resource.NewQuantity(10, resource.DecimalSI)),\n\t\t\t\t}},\n\t\t}\n\t\tscache.AddNode(logger, &node)\n\t\tnodes = append(nodes, &node)\n\t\tobjects = append(objects, &node)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create expected failure reasons for all the nodes. Hopefully they will get rolled up into a non-spammy summary.\n\tfailedNodeStatues := framework.NodeToStatusMap{}\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tfailedNodeStatues[node.Name] = framework.NewStatus(\n\t\t\tframework.Unschedulable,\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Insufficient %v\", v1.ResourceCPU),\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Insufficient %v\", v1.ResourceMemory),\n\t\t).WithPlugin(noderesources.Name)\n\t}\n\tfns := []tf.RegisterPluginFunc{\n\t\ttf.RegisterQueueSortPlugin(queuesort.Name, queuesort.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterBindPlugin(defaultbinder.Name, defaultbinder.New),\n\t\ttf.RegisterPluginAsExtensions(noderesources.Name, frameworkruntime.FactoryAdapter(feature.Features{}, noderesources.NewFit), \"Filter\", \"PreFilter\"),\n\t}\n\n\tinformerFactory := informers.NewSharedInformerFactory(clientsetfake.NewSimpleClientset(objects...), 0)\n\tscheduler, _, errChan := setupTestScheduler(ctx, t, queuedPodStore, scache, informerFactory, nil, fns...)\n\n\tqueuedPodStore.Add(podWithTooBigResourceRequests)\n\tscheduler.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\tselect {\n\tcase err := <-errChan:\n\t\texpectErr := &framework.FitError{\n\t\t\tPod:         podWithTooBigResourceRequests,\n\t\t\tNumAllNodes: len(nodes),\n\t\t\tDiagnosis: framework.Diagnosis{\n\t\t\t\tNodeToStatusMap:      failedNodeStatues,\n\t\t\t\tUnschedulablePlugins: sets.New(noderesources.Name),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(fmt.Sprint(expectErr)) > 150 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"message is too spammy ! %v \", len(fmt.Sprint(expectErr)))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !reflect.DeepEqual(expectErr, err) {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"\\n err \\nWANT=%+v,\\nGOT=%+v\", expectErr, err)\n\t\t}\n\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"timeout after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerWithVolumeBinding(t *testing.T) {\n\tfindErr := fmt.Errorf(\"find string\n\t\tvolumeBinderConfig *volumebinding.FakeVolumeBinderConfig\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"all bound\",\n\t\t\tvolumeBinderConfig: &volumebinding.FakeVolumeBinderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tAllBound: \"FailedScheduling\",\n\t\t\texpectError:        fmt.Errorf(\"running PreBind plugin %q: %w\", volumebinding.Name, bindErr),\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, item := range table {\n\t\tt.Run(item.name, func(t bindingChan, errChan := setupTestSchedulerWithVolumeBinding(ctx, t, fakeVolumeBinder, eventBroadcaster)\n\t\t\teventChan := make(chan struct{})\n\t\t\tstopFunc, err := eventBroadcaster.StartEventWatcher(func(obj runtime.Object) {\n\t\t\t\te, _ := obj.(*eventsv1.Event)\n\t\t\t\tif e, a := item.eventReason, e.Reason; e != a {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected %v, got %v\", e, a)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tclose(eventChan)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ts.ScheduleOne(ctx)\n\t\t\t// Wait for pod to succeed or fail scheduling\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-eventChan:\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(wait.ForeverTestTimeout):\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"scheduling timeout after %v\", wait.ForeverTestTimeout)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tstopFunc()\n\t\t\t// Wait for scheduling to return an error or succeed binding.\n\t\t\tvar (\n\t\t\t\tgotErr  error\n\t\t\t\tgotBind *v1.Binding\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase gotErr = <-errChan:\n\t\t\tcase gotBind = <-bindingChan:\n\t\t\tcase <-time.After(chanTimeout):\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"did not receive pod binding or error after %v\", chanTimeout)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif item.expectError != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif gotErr == nil || item.expectError.Error() != gotErr.Error() {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err !cmp.Equal(item.expectPodBind, gotBind) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"err \\nWANT=%+v,\\nGOT=%+v\", item.expectPodBind, gotBind)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif item.expectAssumeCalled != fakeVolumeBinder.AssumeCalled {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expectedAssumeCall %v\", item.expectAssumeCalled)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif item.expectBindCalled != fakeVolumeBinder.BindCalled {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expectedBindCall %v\", item.expectBindCalled)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestSchedulerBinding(t *testing.T) {\n\ttable := []struct {\n\t\tpodName      string\n\t\textenders    []framework.Extender\n\t\twantBinderID int\n\t\tname         string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"the extender is not a binder\",\n\t\t\tpodName: \"pod0\",\n\t\t\textenders: []framework.Extender{\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: false, interestedPodName: \"pod0\"},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantBinderID: -1, // default binding.\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"one of the extenders is a binder and interested in pod\",\n\t\t\tpodName: \"pod0\",\n\t\t\textenders: []framework.Extender{\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: false, interestedPodName: \"pod0\"},\n\t\t\t\t&fakeExtender{isBinder: true, interestedPodName: show that you:\n//   - get the same priority for a zero-request pod as for a pod with the defaults requests,\n//     both when the zero-request pod is already on the node and when the zero-request pod\n//     is the one being scheduled.\n//   - don't get the same score no matter what we schedule.\nfunc TestZeroRequest(t *testing.T) {\n\t// A pod with no resources. noResources\n\tnoResources1.NodeName = \"node1\"\n\t// A pod with the same resources as a 0-request pod gets by default as its resources (for spreading).\n\tsmall := v1.PodSpec{\n\t\tContainers:
